response.justification: Sarah Smith's background is almost entirely in TypeScript, Node.js, and D language, with no meaningful exposure to Kotlin, the Spring ecosystem, or JVM internals. While Java appears in her skills list and she did C/C++ IDE work at National Instruments, neither translates to the deep JVM and enterprise framework expertise this role requires. Her developer tooling work (test runners, MCP frameworks) is tangentially relevant but insufficient to compensate for the fundamental mismatch in the core technical stack.
strengths: Demonstrated interest in developer tooling and language ecosystem libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial test runner, NI LabWindows IDE contribution)
Strong software craftsmanship practices including TDD, CI/CD, and trunk-based development across multiple roles
Broad systems experience spanning embedded, high-throughput microservices, and full-stack development shows adaptability
concerns: No Kotlin experience whatsoever; Java appears only as a peripheral skill, not a core professional focus
Zero exposure to Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework
No evidence of JVM internals knowledge, IDE plugin development, or Java/Kotlin language tooling work

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework experience
